steps.title: Find the intercept value
steps.content:
The $y$-intercept occurs when $x=0$. Evaluate the original function at that input:
$$f(0)=5^{-4(0+1)}$$
$$f(0)=5^{-4}$$
$$f(0)=\frac{1}{625}$$

steps.title: Rewrite the function
steps.content:
Separate the exponent so the intercept value appears as a coefficient:
$$5^{-4(x+1)}=5^{-4x-4}$$
$$5^{-4x-4}=5^{-4}\cdot5^{-4x}$$
$$5^{-4}\cdot5^{-4x}=\frac{1}{625}(\frac{1}{5})^{4x}$$

steps.title: Pick the displayed form
steps.content: The equivalent form that displays the $y$-intercept value as the coefficient is $f(x)=\frac{1}{625}(\frac{1}{5})^{4x}$. The answer is A.
